title: Nonce Reuse Attack
description: Reusing the same Schnorr nonce across different signatures lets observers solve for the signer private key from public signature data. It is a narrow signing failure mode that explains why nonce generation hygiene matters in wallets and multisig protocols.

resources.title: Nonce Reuse Attack
resources.notes: Decoding walkthrough showing how reused Schnorr nonces expose the private key across two signatures.

resources.title: BIP 340: Schnorr Signatures for secp256k1
resources.notes: Primary specification for BIP340 signing, including nonce derivation context and why accidental nonce reuse can leak the secret key.

resources.title: Programming Bitcoin
resources.notes: Signature-level background for nonce use, key leakage failure modes, and Bitcoin script verification context.
